new_classes.definition_en: Spirituous beverage classified under CN heading 2208 70, produced by flavouring ethyl alcohol or a distillate with natural or artificial flavouring substances, and typically sweetened with sugar, honey, fruits, plants or other agricultural products. Includes cordials and crèmes.

new_classes.label_de: Likör in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 L oder weniger (Unterposition 2208 70 10)
new_classes.definition_en: Liqueur classified under CN subheading 2208 70 10, presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres.

new_classes.label_en: Liqueur production process (compounding of ethyl alcohol or distillate with flavourings and sweetening matter)
new_classes.label_de: Likörherstellungsprozess (Mischung von Ethylalkohol oder Destillat mit Aromen und Süßungsmitteln)
new_classes.definition_en: Process of producing liqueur by combining ethyl alcohol or a distillate with natural or artificial flavouring substances and sweetening matter such as sugar, honey, fruits, plants or other agricultural products.

new_classes.label_en: Sweetened spirit quality (liqueur: spirit with added sweetening and flavouring)
new_classes.label_de: Gesüßte Spirituosenqualität (Likör: Spirituose mit zugesetztem Süßungsmittel und Aromastoffen)
new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by a spirituous beverage that has been sweetened (e.g. with sugar, honey or other sweetening matter) and flavoured, distinguishing liqueurs from unsweetened spirits under heading 2208.

coverage_explanation: The legal note for 22087010 is brief, labelling the subheading as "Likör" (liqueur) and referring to the HS Explanatory Notes for heading 2208, first paragraph B and third paragraph. These HS notes describe liqueurs as spirituous beverages produced by flavouring ethyl alcohol or a distillate with natural or artificial flavouring substances and sweetening matter (sugar, honey, fruits, plants, etc.). The key distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) subclass of spirits beverages under heading 2208 (via parent class SpiritsBeverages2208); (2) sweetened and flavoured quality (SweetenedSpiritQuality and SweetenedOrFlavouredQuality restrictions); (3) production process (LiqueurProductionProcess); (4) container size ≤ 2 L for subheading 22087010 (via SmallContainer BFO part restriction). Coverage is reduced from 1.0 because: the HS note refers to specific flavouring ingredient types (fruits, plants, herbs, cream, etc.) and minimum sugar content thresholds for EU liqueur definitions which cannot be fully captured without additional quality/quantity classes for sugar content; the note is a cross-reference and the detailed content of HS para B and third paragraph is not directly provided in the legal text supplied.
